title: 130  Can a devotee without knowledge of Gita get Krishna
speaker: HH Stoka Krishna Maharaj

segments.text: The realization part you are talking about, that example where one South Indian Brahmana
is who doesn't know to read, he keeps Bhagavad Gita and Chaitanya Mahaprabhu appreciates.
So does he have the realization of whole Bhagavad Gita, means the knowledge is included, although
he has not read Bhagavad Gita?
No, in that case, you see there is a sum and substance of all knowledge, that is devotion
to Krishna, surrender to Krishna.
So knowledge leads one to that, knowledge is not an end in itself.
So if the person fortunately has gone to the end without all this knowledge, he has all
the final knowledge he has got.
So therefore that is real realization.
So he is already, what would you do after understanding Bhagavad Gita?
The strongest message is to surrender to him.
The strongest message is, it's a dialogue between, it's a loving dialogue between the
Supreme Lord and his devotee.
So now he already understood that, essence of it.
So therefore, you know, he had realization.
By the Lord's incarnation.
Similarly, one may not know too much information, too much knowledge about this Shastra inside
out, and everything, but by serving Prabhupad and Krishna, by the mercy of the pure devotee,
he can get an, inkling of eternal relationship with Krishna, His loving relationship.
He can develop love for Krishna, without knowing all these things.
In his practical life, he can experience that.
That is a gift, it's Bohri's gift.
It's a gift given to the devotee because he can feel for Krishna.
It's not easy to feel for Krishna always.
It is not easy to feel gratitude to Krishna always.
It is not easy to feel Krishna's kindness always.
It's not easy because material energy is always bombarding and it's the other way around.
The three modes work the other way around.
So if over and above that, if we are fortunate, then we will have attachment to Krishna.
We will have, you know, this kind of feelings for Krishna.
Personal relationship with the Supreme Lord is possible even without full realization.
That is Bhakti Yoga.
Directly we are going to relationship with Krishna through Prabhupada's process of devotional service.
